title: datura innoxia subsp. quinquecuspidata
intro: Datura innoxia subsp. quinquecuspidata, also known as Devil's Trumpet or Jimsonweed, is a subspecies of the Datura innoxia plant, a type of poisonous flowering plant native to the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antispasmodic, anesthetize the respiratory system, and lower body temperature. The health benefits of Datura innoxia subsp. quinquecuspidata include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity. Herbal preparations of Datura innoxia subsp. quinquecuspidata can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedy.
